{{wikidata link|Q54153278}} == Japanese content == '''Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine''' (かがみつくりいたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in Shiroshita District of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and in adjoining settlements of the same town there are two shrines regarded as candidate sites. === Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine (Miyako) === {{神社 |名称=Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine |画像=[[File:Kahamitsukuri Ita Shaden.jpg|270px]]
Shrine buildings
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 30 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 58 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Nara |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}Miyako, Aza Hoya 60 |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ishikori-dome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) candidate shrine
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} }} === Overview === It is enshrined at the south end of the Miyako settlement, west of Miyako Pond. Miyako belonged to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiroshita District|1=UNKNOWN}} since ancient times; while early history is unknown, it is thought that the Miyake no Muraji clan of this area enshrined their ancestral deity. It was formerly called Hoya Myōjin. === Precinct === The current shrine buildings were rebuilt after typhoon damage in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1997|Q2088|WD=Q2088|1=1997}}). The honden is an ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with chigi and katsuogi. ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Ita Miyako Torii.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Ita Miyako Honden.jpg|Honden === Local information === ; Location *{{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}Miyako, Aza Hoya 60 ; Access * About 15 minutes on foot northwest from {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Station|Q7689224|WD=Q7689224|1=Tawaramoto Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashihara Line|1=UNKNOWN}} === External links === *[https://narayado.info/nara/kagamitsukuri-ita.html Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine @ Miyako, Tawaramoto] === Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine (Hozu) === {{神社 |名称=Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine (Hozu) |画像= {{画像募集中|cat=磯城郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|33|24|N|135|46|56|E}}}} |位置 = {{coord|34|33|24|N|135|46|56|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine (Hozu)}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=24|lon_deg=135|lon_min=46|lon_sec=56|label=Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine (Hozu)|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}Hozu 150| |祭神={{ill|lt=Ishikori-dome-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) candidate shrine
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿={{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 13|Q2925|WD=Q2925|1=October 13}} }} === Overview === It is located at the southwest edge of the Hozu ring-moat settlement. Although the current site belongs to former {{ill|lt=Tōichi District|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036|1=Toichi district}}, before the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}} it is said to have been in Ita-shiki, about 300 m east of the present location, and moved here along with relocation of the Hozu settlement. In ancient times, the Hozu clan of Mononobe surname is said to have lived in the ring-moat settlement, and this shrine is inferred to have worshiped their ancestral deity. === Precinct === The honden consists of two buildings, north and south honden. The south honden was built in the rare "sumiki-iri {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}" method during the {{ill|lt=Bunka (era)|Q1373146|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} years (1804-1818). === Local information === ; Location *{{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}Hozu 150 ; Access * About 10 minutes on foot west from {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Station|Q7689224|WD=Q7689224|1=Tawaramoto Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashihara Line|1=UNKNOWN}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かかみつくりいたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿={{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 13|Q2925|WD=Q2925|1=October 13}} }} === Overview === It is located at the southwest edge of the Hozu ring-moat settlement. Although the current site belongs to former {{ill|lt=Tōichi District|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036|1=Toichi district}}, before the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}} it is said to have been in Ita-shiki, about 300 m east of the present location, and moved here along with relocation of the Hozu settlement. In ancient times, the Hozu clan of Mononobe surname is said to have lived in the ring-moat settlement, and this shrine is inferred to have worshiped their ancestral deity. === Precinct === The honden consists of two buildings, north and south honden. The south honden was built in the rare "sumiki-iri {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}" method during the {{ill|lt=Bunka (era)|Q1373146|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} years (1804-1818). === Local information === ; Location *{{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}Hozu 150 ; Access * About 10 minutes on foot west from {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Station|Q7689224|WD=Q7689224|1=Tawaramoto Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashihara Line|1=UNKNOWN}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in Shiroshita District of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and in adjoining settlements of the same town there are two shrines regarded as candidate sites. === Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine (Miyako) === {{神社 |名称=Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine |画像=[[File:Kahamitsukuri Ita Shaden.jpg|270px]]
Shrine buildings

Miyako belonged to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiroshita District|1=UNKNOWN}} since ancient times; while early history is unknown, it is thought that the Miyake no Muraji clan of this area enshrined their ancestral deity. It was formerly called Hoya Myōjin. === Precinct === The current shrine buildings were rebuilt after typhoon damage in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1997|Q2088|WD=Q2088|1=1997}}). The honden is an ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with chigi and katsuogi. ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Ita Miyako Torii.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Ita Miyako Honden.jpg|Honden === Local information === ; Location *{{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}Miyako, Aza Hoya 60 ; Access * About 15 minutes on foot northwest from {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Station|Q7689224|WD=Q7689224|1=Tawaramoto Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashihara Line|1=UNKNOWN}} === External links === *[https://narayado.info/nara/kagamitsukuri-ita.html Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine @ Miyako, Tawaramoto] === Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine (Hozu) === {{神社 |名称=Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine (Hozu) |画像= {{画像募集中|cat=磯城郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|33|24|N|135|46|56|E}}}} |位置 = {{coord|34|33|24|N|135|46|56|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine (Hozu)}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=24|lon_deg=135|lon_min=46|lon_sec=56|label=Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine (Hozu)|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}Hozu 150| |祭神={{ill|lt=Ishikori-dome-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) candidate shrine

Tawaramoto-chō, Shiki-gun, Nara-ken 636-0302', 'language': 'en'} * {'text': '奈良県磯城郡田原本町保津', 'language': 'ja'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Kagamitsukuri Ita Miyako Torii.jpg [[JA:鏡作伊多神社]] {{wikidata link|Q54153278}} == Japanese content == '''Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine''' (かがみつくりいたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in Shiroshita District of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and in adjoining settlements of the same town there are two shrines regarded as candidate sites. === Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine (Miyako) === {{神社 |名称=Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine |画像=[[File:Kahamitsukuri Ita Shaden.jpg|270px]]
Shrine buildings
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 30 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 58 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Nara |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}Miyako, Aza Hoya 60 |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ishikori-dome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) candidate shrine
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} }} === Overview === It is enshrined at the south end of the Miyako settlement, west of Miyako Pond. Miyako belonged to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiroshita District|1=UNKNOWN}} since ancient times; while early history is unknown, it is thought that the Miyake no Muraji clan of this area enshrined their ancestral deity. It was formerly called Hoya Myōjin. === Precinct === The current shrine buildings were rebuilt after typhoon damage in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1997|Q2088|WD=Q2088|1=1997}}). The honden is an ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with chigi and katsuogi. ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Ita Miyako Torii.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Ita Miyako Honden.jpg|Honden === Local information === ; Location *{{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}Miyako, Aza Hoya 60 ; Access * About 15 minutes on foot northwest from {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Station|Q7689224|WD=Q7689224|1=Tawaramoto Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashihara Line|1=UNKNOWN}} === External links === *[https://narayado.info/nara/kagamitsukuri-ita.html Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine @ Miyako, Tawaramoto] === Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine (Hozu) === {{神社 |名称=Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine (Hozu) |画像= {{画像募集中|cat=磯城郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|33|24|N|135|46|56|E}}}} |位置 = {{coord|34|33|24|N|135|46|56|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine (Hozu)}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=24|lon_deg=135|lon_min=46|lon_sec=56|label=Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine (Hozu)|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}Hozu 150| |祭神={{ill|lt=Ishikori-dome-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) candidate shrine
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿={{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 13|Q2925|WD=Q2925|1=October 13}} }} === Overview === It is located at the southwest edge of the Hozu ring-moat settlement. Although the current site belongs to former {{ill|lt=Tōichi District|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036|1=Toichi district}}, before the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}} it is said to have been in Ita-shiki, about 300 m east of the present location, and moved here along with relocation of the Hozu settlement. In ancient times, the Hozu clan of Mononobe surname is said to have lived in the ring-moat settlement, and this shrine is inferred to have worshiped their ancestral deity. === Precinct === The honden consists of two buildings, north and south honden. The south honden was built in the rare "sumiki-iri {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}" method during the {{ill|lt=Bunka (era)|Q1373146|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} years (1804-1818). === Local information === ; Location *{{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}Hozu 150 ; Access * About 10 minutes on foot west from {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Station|Q7689224|WD=Q7689224|1=Tawaramoto Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashihara Line|1=UNKNOWN}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{wikidata link|Q54153278}} == Japanese content == '''Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine''' (かがみつくりいたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in Shiroshita District of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and in adjoining settlements of the same town there are two shrines regarded as candidate sites. === Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine (Miyako) === {{神社 |名称=Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine |画像=[[File:Kahamitsukuri Ita Shaden.jpg|270px]]
Shrine buildings
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 30 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 58 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Nara |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}Miyako, Aza Hoya 60 |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ishikori-dome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) candidate shrine
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} }} === Overview === It is enshrined at the south end of the Miyako settlement, west of Miyako Pond. Miyako belonged to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiroshita District|1=UNKNOWN}} since ancient times; while early history is unknown, it is thought that the Miyake no Muraji clan of this area enshrined their ancestral deity. It was formerly called Hoya Myōjin. === Precinct === The current shrine buildings were rebuilt after typhoon damage in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1997|Q2088|WD=Q2088|1=1997}}). The honden is an ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with chigi and katsuogi. ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Ita Miyako Torii.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Ita Miyako Honden.jpg|Honden === Local information === ; Location *{{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}Miyako, Aza Hoya 60 ; Access * About 15 minutes on foot northwest from {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Station|Q7689224|WD=Q7689224|1=Tawaramoto Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashihara Line|1=UNKNOWN}} === External links === *[https://narayado.info/nara/kagamitsukuri-ita.html Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine @ Miyako, Tawaramoto] === Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine (Hozu) === {{神社 |名称=Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine (Hozu) |画像= {{画像募集中|cat=磯城郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|33|24|N|135|46|56|E}}}} |位置 = {{coord|34|33|24|N|135|46|56|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine (Hozu)}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=24|lon_deg=135|lon_min=46|lon_sec=56|label=Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine (Hozu)|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}Hozu 150| |祭神={{ill|lt=Ishikori-dome-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) candidate shrine
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿={{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 13|Q2925|WD=Q2925|1=October 13}} }} === Overview === It is located at the southwest edge of the Hozu ring-moat settlement. Although the current site belongs to former {{ill|lt=Tōichi District|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036|1=Toichi district}}, before the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}} it is said to have been in Ita-shiki, about 300 m east of the present location, and moved here along with relocation of the Hozu settlement. In ancient times, the Hozu clan of Mononobe surname is said to have lived in the ring-moat settlement, and this shrine is inferred to have worshiped their ancestral deity. === Precinct === The honden consists of two buildings, north and south honden. The south honden was built in the rare "sumiki-iri {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}" method during the {{ill|lt=Bunka (era)|Q1373146|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} years (1804-1818). === Local information === ; Location *{{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}Hozu 150 ; Access * About 10 minutes on foot west from {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Station|Q7689224|WD=Q7689224|1=Tawaramoto Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashihara Line|1=UNKNOWN}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tawaramoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

The honden is an ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with chigi and katsuogi. ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Ita Miyako Torii.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Ita Miyako Honden.jpg|Honden === Local information === ; Location *{{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}Miyako, Aza Hoya 60 ; Access * About 15 minutes on foot northwest from {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Station|Q7689224|WD=Q7689224|1=Tawaramoto Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashihara Line|1=UNKNOWN}} === External links === *[https://narayado.info/nara/kagamitsukuri-ita.html Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine @ Miyako, Tawaramoto] === Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine (Hozu) === {{神社 |名称=Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine (Hozu) |画像= {{画像募集中|cat=磯城郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|33|24|N|135|46|56|E}}}} |位置 = {{coord|34|33|24|N|135|46|56|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine (Hozu)}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=24|lon_deg=135|lon_min=46|lon_sec=56|label=Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine (Hozu)|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}Hozu 150| |祭神={{ill|lt=Ishikori-dome-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) candidate shrine
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿={{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 13|Q2925|WD=Q2925|1=October 13}} }} === Overview === It is located at the southwest edge of the Hozu ring-moat settlement. Although the current site belongs to former {{ill|lt=Tōichi District|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036|1=Toichi district}}, before the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}} it is said to have been in Ita-shiki, about 300 m east of the present location, and moved here along with relocation of the Hozu settlement. In ancient times, the Hozu clan of Mononobe surname is said to have lived in the ring-moat settlement, and this shrine is inferred to have worshiped their ancestral deity. === Precinct === The honden consists of two buildings, north and south honden. The south honden was built in the rare "sumiki-iri {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}" method during the {{ill|lt=Bunka (era)|Q1373146|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} years (1804-1818). === Local information === ; Location *{{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}Hozu 150 ; Access * About 10 minutes on foot west from {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Station|Q7689224|WD=Q7689224|1=Tawaramoto Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashihara Line|1=UNKNOWN}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tawaramoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]]

Miyako, Tawaramoto-chō, Shiki-gun, Nara-ken 636-0302', 'language': 'en'} * {'text': '奈良県磯城郡田原本町保津', 'language': 'ja'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Kagamitsukuri Ita Miyako Torii.jpg [[JA:鏡作伊多神社]] {{wikidata link|Q54153278}} == Japanese content == '''Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine''' (かがみつくりいたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|qid=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|qid=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} in Shiroshita District of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|qid=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and in adjoining settlements of the same town there are two shrines regarded as candidate sites. === Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine (Miyako) === {{神社 |名称=Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine |画像=[[File:Kahamitsukuri Ita Shaden.jpg|270px]]
Shrine buildings
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 30 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 58 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Nara |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|qid=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|qid=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}Miyako, Aza Hoya 60 |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ishikori-dome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small) candidate shrine
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|qid=Q2923|1=October 9}} }} === Overview === It is enshrined at the south end of the Miyako settlement, west of Miyako Pond. Miyako belonged to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiroshita District|1=UNKNOWN}} since ancient times; while early history is unknown, it is thought that the Miyake no Muraji clan of this area enshrined their ancestral deity. It was formerly called Hoya Myōjin. === Precinct === The current shrine buildings were rebuilt after typhoon damage in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1997|Q2088|qid=Q2088|1=1997}}). The honden is an ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with chigi and katsuogi. ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Ita Miyako Torii.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Ita Miyako Honden.jpg|Honden === Local information === ; Location *{{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|qid=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|qid=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}Miyako, Aza Hoya 60 ; Access * About 15 minutes on foot northwest from {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Station|Q7689224|qid=Q7689224|1=Tawaramoto Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|qid=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashihara Line|1=UNKNOWN}} === External links === *[https://narayado.info/nara/kagamitsukuri-ita.html Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine @ Miyako, Tawaramoto] === Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine (Hozu) === {{神社 |名称=Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine (Hozu) |画像= {{画像募集中|cat=磯城郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|33|24|N|135|46|56|E}}}} |位置 = {{coord|34|33|24|N|135|46|56|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine (Hozu)}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=24|lon_deg=135|lon_min=46|lon_sec=56|label=Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine (Hozu)|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|qid=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|qid=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}Hozu 150| |祭神={{ill|lt=Ishikori-dome-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small) candidate shrine
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿={{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|qid=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 13|Q2925|qid=Q2925|1=October 13}} }} === Overview === It is located at the southwest edge of the Hozu ring-moat settlement. Although the current site belongs to former {{ill|lt=Tōichi District|Q11405036|qid=Q11405036|1=Toichi district}}, before the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|qid=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}} it is said to have been in Ita-shiki, about 300 m east of the present location, and moved here along with relocation of the Hozu settlement. In ancient times, the Hozu clan of Mononobe surname is said to have lived in the ring-moat settlement, and this shrine is inferred to have worshiped their ancestral deity. === Precinct === The honden consists of two buildings, north and south honden. The south honden was built in the rare "sumiki-iri {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|qid=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}" method during the {{ill|lt=Bunka (era)|Q1373146|qid=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} years (1804-1818). === Local information === ; Location *{{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|qid=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|qid=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}Hozu 150 ; Access * About 10 minutes on foot west from {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Station|Q7689224|qid=Q7689224|1=Tawaramoto Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|qid=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashihara Line|1=UNKNOWN}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tawaramoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]]
